title: Bankot Fort
tagline: Bankot Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Bankot Fort / Himmatgad Fort/ Fort Victoria is a fort located 47 km from Dapoli, in Ratnagiri district, of Maharashtra.
overview.description: Bankot Fort / Himmatgad Fort/ Fort Victoria is a fort located 47 km from Dapoli, in Ratnagiri district, of Maharashtra. This fort is an important fort in Ratnagiri district. The Fort is located on a prominent and commanding point for guarding the trade route along the Savitri River, which runs till, Mahad which was a busy route in medieval times. It is a fort located on a hill near the sea.

topPlaces.name: Savitri River
topPlaces.description: Savitri River is one of the five rivers which originate from Mahabaleshwar in Maharashtra state in India. It originates at Savitri point near Mahabaleshwar and flows through the Bankot village and directly goes to the Arabian Sea Raigad district and eventually meets Arabian Sea at Harehareshwar.

topPlaces.name: Bankot
topPlaces.description: Bankot is a town in Maharashtra, India. It became part of the Bombay Presidency in 1756.

topPlaces.name: Velas, Maharashtra
topPlaces.description: Velas is a village in Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra, India. It is noted as a birthplace of Nana Phadnis, a n influential statesman of the Maratha Empire.

topPlaces.name: Harihareshwar
topPlaces.description: Harihareshwar is a town in Raigad district, in Maharashtra, India. It is surrounded by three hills named Harihareshwar, Harshinachal and Pushpadri.

topPlaces.name: Shrivardhan
topPlaces.description: Shrivardhan (also spelled Shriwardhan) is a subdistrict/upazila/tehsil, city, and local governing body in the Raigad district of the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is renowned as the birthplace of Balaji Vishwanath, the first Peshwa (1713–1720) and father of Bajirao I, who held the position of Deshmukh in the town.

topPlaces.name: Rowale
topPlaces.description: Rowale is a small village in Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra state in Western India. The 2011 Census of India recorded a total of 453 residents in the village.

topPlaces.name: Ade, Maharashtra
topPlaces.description: Ade is a small village in Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra state in Western India. The 2011 Census of India recorded 1,718 residents in the village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bankot Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bankot Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bankot Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (115 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (107 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bankot Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bankot Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bankot Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Bankot Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bankot Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Savitri River, Bankot, Velas, Maharashtra, Harihareshwar, Shrivardhan.
